title: dryopteris subexaltata
intro: Dryopteris subexaltata, also known as the sub-exalted wood fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the Dryopteris genus and is native to the tropical forests of Central and South America. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which have been attributed to the presence of flavonoids and other compounds found in its leaves. The health benefits of Dryopteris subexaltata include reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health, as well as acting as an antiseptic and antimicrobial agent. The herbal preparations of Dryopteris subexaltata include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a variety of health conditions, including fever, respiratory issues, and gastrointestinal problems.
